actions.description: Pantry-staple state is modeled on group-scoped IngredientFoodModel.is_pantry_staple, so one household marking flour as a staple silently affects every other household in the group. This directly contradicts input §5 multitenant-isolation requirement and the household-facing language in US-4/US-7.
actions.rationale: Three independent axes (architecture, completeness, consistency) flag the same root cause: the spec selected a group-scoped column for what input.md:73-75 requires to be a per-household marker. NC-001 is currently 'recommended default = group-scoped', which is incompatible with the required behavior; shipping as-is would cause cross-household behavioral leakage and fail the mandated multitenant test.
actions.suggested_action: Resolve NC-001 BEFORE coding: add a household-food association table (parallel to households_to_ingredient_foods in mealie/db/models/recipe/ingredient.py:157-162) — e.g. household_pantry_staples(household_id, food_id) — and route the pantry-filter predicate through this scope. Update schemas/routes accordingly and add a test where households A and B in the same group set different staple state for the same food and observe no bleed-through.

actions.description: The CAS marker (last_auto_synced_at) is acquired before sync work, but Mealie repository methods commit inside bulk_create_items / add_recipe_ingredients_to_list. A mid-sync failure can leave the household marked synced with incomplete writes, and no-op runs (no meal plan / missing list) also burn the daily slot — contradicting the explicit edge-case rules that say no-op must not bump the marker.
actions.rationale: Three reviewers converge: architecture flags the commit-in-repository pattern (mealie/repos/repository_generic.py:195-203), executability flags FR-010 vs edge-cases timing contradiction, consistency flags both the day-boundary mismatch (FR-008 vs FR-010) and the CAS-before-work no-op problem. This is the canonical 'marker-before-work' idempotency bug and is blocking.
actions.suggested_action: Specify exact ordering: (1) resolve target list ownership and load today's meal plan first; (2) if no plan or no resolvable list, log + skip without touching marker; (3) wrap item-creation + recipe-reference + event dispatch in a single controlled transaction; (4) only after all writes succeed, set last_auto_synced_at via conditional UPDATE (with WHERE last_auto_synced_at IS NULL OR last_auto_synced_at < :scheduled_local_day). Add an explicit failure-injection test proving a mid-sync exception leaves the marker untouched so the next run retries.

actions.description: The required per-household auto_sync_run_time (HH:MM, default '00:00') field is missing; the scheduler is registered as MINUTES_5 instead of every 30 minutes; the 'today' window is hardcoded to midnight; field naming diverges from input (auto_sync_meal_plan_to_shopping_list vs auto_sync_meal_plan_to_shopping); and the FR-008 30-min-before-midnight predicate is incompatible with the FR-010 today-midnight CAS predicate.
actions.rationale: Four axes flag overlapping aspects of one root design defect: the spec omitted auto_sync_run_time and then derived inconsistent gating logic. Input §1 (input.md:17-23) and §2 (input.md:30-38) make the field set and 30-minute cadence explicit. Without this fix, scheduler tests, PATCH support, migration, and window semantics are all wrong.
actions.suggested_action: Add auto_sync_run_time: str (HH:MM, default '00:00', validated). Register the task via the 30-minute scheduler bucket (or document the supported equivalent). Compute scheduled_local_instant = today_in_household_tz at auto_sync_run_time; gate execution to runs whose now() falls within [scheduled_local_instant, scheduled_local_instant + 30min). Unify the idempotency predicate to compare last_auto_synced_at against scheduled_local_instant's local date. Restore the input field name (or document a compatibility alias). Drop the extra auto_sync_pantry_filter_enabled flag (see META-005).

actions.description: A configured auto_sync_target_shopping_list_id is passed directly to ShoppingListService.add_recipe_ingredients_to_list, which creates ShoppingListItem rows for the supplied list_id without verifying household ownership. A stale or maliciously-set cross-household id will write into another household's list, defeating multitenant isolation (US-6 / SC-006).
actions.rationale: Architecture and consistency both surface this: scoped repos protect the fallback path (FR-019) but NOT a pre-configured target id. Mealie's shopping-list service writes items before reading the list through the scoped repo, so the scoped-repo defense doesn't apply automatically.
actions.suggested_action: Add validation at TWO seams: (a) PATCH /households/preferences must reject auto_sync_target_shopping_list_id values that do not belong to the current household; (b) sync task must call repos.group_shopping_lists.get_one(target_id) via the household-scoped repository factory and abort (with the no-target-list i18n message) if missing. Add a multitenant test where household A configures B's list id and asserts zero rows change in B's list.

actions.description: Pantry filtering is incoherent: spec adds an optional auto_sync_pantry_filter_enabled gate (input requires unconditional filtering); FR-011 passes recipe_ingredients=None which delegates fetching to the existing helper and leaves nothing to pre-filter; FR-012 says pre-filter the list; sub-recipe (referenced recipe) expansion at shopping_lists.py:343-350 is not addressed.
actions.rationale: Three axes flag the same implementation seam contradiction. Without a single concrete pipeline, the coder will either (a) skip filtering entirely because recipe_ingredients=None, (b) filter top-level only and let sub-recipe staples leak, or (c) implement something inconsistent with the test suite.
actions.suggested_action: Remove auto_sync_pantry_filter_enabled — make pantry-staple filtering unconditional for auto-sync per input.md:41-44. Pin one implementation: fetch each recipe (and recursively expand referenced recipes) into a fully-materialized ingredient list, apply the per-household pantry-staple predicate from META-001, then call add_recipe_ingredients_to_list with an explicit recipe_ingredients= filtered list (NOT None). Cite get_shopping_list_items_from_recipe at L323-340 and the recursive expansion at L343-350. Add SC + test for sub-recipe staple filtering.

actions.description: Input §2 requires a NEW event MealPlanAutoSyncedToShopping with safe payload {household_id, shopping_list_id, added_item_count, skipped_pantry_count}. Spec instead dispatches the generic EventTypes.shopping_list_updated with EventShoppingListData carrying only shopping_list_id, and doesn't specify the required EventOperation field, so subscribers cannot distinguish auto-sync from any other list update.
actions.rationale: Three axes converge. The latent tension between ARCH-H-1 (new event type) and EXEC-H-003 (set operation on existing EventShoppingListData) resolves cleanly in favor of input's explicit naming requirement: add the new event type and still include the required operation field.
actions.suggested_action: Add EventTypes.mealplan_auto_synced_to_shopping in mealie/services/event_bus_service/event_types.py and define class EventMealPlanAutoSyncedData(EventDocumentDataBase) with operation: EventOperation, household_id: UUID4, shopping_list_id: UUID4, added_item_count: int, skipped_pantry_count: int — and NO recipe/ingredient/user-identifying fields. Update subscriber registration migration if needed. Cite EventDocumentDataBase at event_types.py:88-91 to anchor the operation field requirement.

actions.description: Manual run-now route, response, authorization, and idempotency semantics are wrong: spec uses /api/households/preferences/auto-sync/run-now (input requires /auto-sync-shopping/run-now), returns a vague localized success payload (input requires {added_count, skipped_pantry_count, target_list_id, run_at}), and reuses the daily-gated sync function so a post-scheduled run-now no-ops — contradicting US-3.
actions.rationale: Completeness and consistency both flag this. Input §3 (input.md:48-53) is unambiguous about the route, the four response fields, household-admin auth, and the explicit 'bypass LastAutoSyncedAt but still update it' semantic.
actions.suggested_action: Use the exact path POST /api/households/preferences/auto-sync-shopping/run-now. Add an explicit household-admin permission check. Define a force=True / manual-mode parameter on the shared sync function that bypasses the daily CAS guard but still writes last_auto_synced_at on success. Return the exact JSON shape {added_count, skipped_pantry_count, target_list_id, run_at} and add SC/tests asserting both the bypass and the count fields.

actions.description: last_auto_synced_at is added to the preference schema AND covered by partial-PATCH semantics with NC-003 treating nullable fields as clearable. A client could clear or backdate the server-owned idempotency marker and break daily-sync guarantees.
actions.rationale: Single reviewer but logically severe: the entire idempotency story in META-002 depends on this field being server-owned. Architecture review's CAS recommendations are moot if a client can null the marker.
actions.suggested_action: Remove last_auto_synced_at from the client-writable PATCH/PUT schemas (HouseholdPreferencesUpdate / partial-update schema in META-012). Keep it in the read schema for diagnostics. Add SC asserting that PATCH attempts to set last_auto_synced_at are ignored or rejected.

actions.description: i18n keys diverge from input AND are inconsistent within the spec: input requires auto-sync.no-meal-plan-today, auto-sync.no-target-list, auto-sync.already-synced-today; spec defines mealplan.auto-sync.success / .no-active-meal-plan / .target-shopping-list-not-found; summary/US-9 use underscore namespace mealplan.auto_sync.* while FR-016 uses hyphen mealplan.auto-sync.*.
actions.rationale: Three axes flag the same drift. Without a single key set, every implementation site and every test fixture will pick a different string.
actions.suggested_action: Adopt the three exact keys from input.md:55-59: auto-sync.no-meal-plan-today, auto-sync.no-target-list, auto-sync.already-synced-today. Pick ONE convention everywhere (recommend the hyphenated unqualified namespace as in input). Update summary, US-9, FR-016, SC-011, and edge cases. Add SC asserting that the three localized error responses use exactly these keys.

actions.description: Input §2 step 3 explicitly requires reusing the existing consolidate_ingredients function (with the case-3 fix). Spec cites bulk_create_items / can_merge / add_recipe_ingredients_to_list but never names or cites consolidate_ingredients as the shared (food_id, unit_id) consolidator.
actions.rationale: Implementation-constraint compliance: input.md:79-81 makes seam reuse mandatory ('必须复用'). Without an explicit citation, the coder may reimplement the merge logic and diverge from case-3's fix.
actions.suggested_action: Locate the consolidate_ingredients function in the Mealie tree, cite its full path:lines, and require its use in FR-011/FR-012. If the canonical equivalent has a different name in the current codebase, document the verified seam and add a test asserting (food_id, unit_id) consolidation parity.

actions.description: Test matrix omits required cases: disabled-scheduled-skip, 204/0-added-no-meal-plan integration, cross-group isolation, pantry-staple cross-household isolation, scheduler-mock 30-minute interval assertion, and exact manual-trigger response-count assertions.
actions.rationale: Input §5 (input.md:60-75) enumerates the test bullets that must be covered; the spec lists generic categories but misses these specific cases. Per the completeness gate these are critical.
actions.suggested_action: Add one SC + one test per missing bullet: (a) unit/integration: disabled household skipped by scheduler; (b) integration: no-meal-plan day returns 204 or 0 added; (c) multitenant: cross-group complete isolation; (d) multitenant: per-household pantry-staple isolation (depends on META-001); (e) scheduler-mock: registration cadence is 30 minutes; (f) integration: manual run-now response contains exact added_count / skipped_pantry_count values.

actions.description: PATCH partial-update semantics are left fuzzy. Mealie's generic update path uses model_dump() (full update), which would reset unspecified preferences to defaults — incompatible with FR-005's exclude_unset intent.
actions.rationale: Without an explicit partial schema, the coder will route PATCH through the existing generic update and silently overwrite untouched fields. Also blocks META-008 (excluding server-owned fields from writes).
actions.suggested_action: Define HouseholdPreferencesPartialUpdate schema with all fields Optional[...] = None, EXCLUDING server-owned last_auto_synced_at. Apply via model_dump(exclude_unset=True) and merge into the loaded row instead of routing through repository_generic.update().

actions.description: FR-019 fallback (when no target id is configured) cites only the ShoppingListItem model; no repository call or SQLAlchemy query is named for 'first household shopping list ordered by created_at'.
actions.rationale: Single reviewer, but blocks executability: without the concrete repo seam, the coder must invent one and may forget household scoping or non-deleted filters.
actions.suggested_action: Cite the exact repo method or query: e.g. repos.group_shopping_lists.query(order_by=ShoppingList.created_at).first() or the equivalent in mealie/repos/repository_shopping_list.py. Cite ShoppingList model at mealie/db/models/household/shopping_list.py:147-181 and the created_at mixin at mealie/db/models/_model_base.py:18-23.

actions.description: Input requires appended items mark recipe_references linking back to meal plan / recipe. Spec relies on existing recipe-id-only references and doesn't state whether meal-plan-entry identity is also persisted (or impossible in the current model).
actions.rationale: Ambiguity here will lead to under-/over-implementation. Either current recipe-only refs satisfy the requirement, or an extras/metadata strategy is needed.
actions.suggested_action: Verify in the model layer whether ShoppingListItem.recipe_references can carry a meal-plan-entry id. If not, either document that recipe-only refs satisfy the spirit of the requirement (with rationale) or specify an extras dict / new column to carry meal_plan_entry_id. Add SC/test accordingly.

actions.description: Input requires appended items merge into existing unchecked same-(food_id, unit_id) rows by accumulating quantity rather than creating duplicates. Spec mentions checked rows are not merged but never explicitly requires the unchecked-accumulation behavior.
actions.rationale: Without an explicit FR + test, the coder may always create new rows and let users see duplicates after auto-sync.
actions.suggested_action: Add FR: 'For each filtered ingredient, if the target list contains an unchecked item with matching (food_id, unit_id), accumulate quantity and preserve/merge recipe_references; otherwise create a new item.' Add SC + integration test exercising both branches.

actions.description: Input §4 requires migration + schema + repo + admin/foods routes for Food.is_pantry_staple. Spec covers model/schema/migration but says no repo method is needed and uses can_organize permission rather than naming an admin-foods route/permission.
actions.rationale: Underspecified permission and route surface will cause the coder to bolt the field onto whatever existing CRUD path exists, possibly without admin gating.
actions.suggested_action: State the intended permission explicitly (e.g. household admin or group admin), name the route(s) where pantry-staple toggle is exposed (admin/foods or similar), and confirm whether a repo method is needed for read/update beyond the generic CRUD. NOTE: this overlaps with META-001 — pantry-staple state should be per-household, so the admin endpoint should write to the household-pantry-staple association, not to a Food column.

actions.description: Several FR citations are wrong or incomplete: FR-019 cites ShoppingListItem instead of ShoppingList for created_at fallback; FR-018 cites only upgrade() of the example migration, missing the downgrade pair; FR-015 cites EventShoppingListData but not the inherited EventOperation field from EventDocumentDataBase.
actions.rationale: Executability gate requires precise citations so the coder hits the right symbols. These mis-citations would route the coder to wrong files or incomplete patterns.
actions.suggested_action: Update code_references for FR-019 to cite mealie/db/models/household/shopping_list.py:147-181 (ShoppingList) and mealie/db/models/_model_base.py:18-23 (created_at). For FR-018 add the downgrade range L35-45 of the announcements migration example. For FR-015 add mealie/services/event_bus_service/event_types.py:88-91 (EventDocumentDataBase.operation). Update both spec.md and spec.json so code_references remain identical.

actions.description: FR↔SC bidirectional links are asymmetric in spec.json (FR-020 lists SC-001/002/003/008 but those SCs don't list FR-020 back) and absent from spec.md (FR entries link only related_user_stories there).
actions.rationale: Low impact on correctness but affects traceability and breaks any downstream tooling that walks the graph in both directions.
actions.suggested_action: Either (a) reciprocate the FR-020 links from SC-001/002/003/008.related_requirements, or (b) drop FR-020's outbound SC links and recast it as a pure test-coverage NFR. Optionally surface FR↔SC links in spec.md to match spec.json field parity.

cross_axis_conflicts: Latent (resolved in META-006): architecture review wants a NEW event type MealPlanAutoSyncedToShopping (ARCH-H-1) while executability review wants the existing EventShoppingListData enriched with an explicit EventOperation field (EXEC-H-003). Resolution: input.md:45-47 mandates the new event name, so add the new EventTypes member and dedicated payload class, AND include the operation field on the new payload (inherited from EventDocumentDataBase).

Latent (resolved in META-002): architecture says set last_auto_synced_at only AFTER all work succeeds (ARCH-C-1) while spec FR-010 says CAS-UPDATE before reading meal plans to claim the slot. Consistency reviewer's compromise (CON-C-002) is canonical: resolve target/meal preconditions first, then perform CAS immediately before the append + event-dispatch transaction. This yields both safety against concurrent replicas and correct no-op semantics.

Latent (resolved in META-005 + META-001): completeness says make pantry filtering unconditional (drop the auto_sync_pantry_filter_enabled gate) while architecture says model pantry-staple state per household. Both are required and compatible — the optional gating flag must go AND the underlying boolean must become household-scoped. The new per-household association table is the single source of truth used by the unconditional filter.

Latent (META-016 vs META-001): completeness flags an under-specified Food.is_pantry_staple admin route, but architecture mandates moving pantry-staple state OFF the Food row entirely. Resolution: implement META-001 first; the admin endpoint should write to the new household-pantry-staple association rather than to a Food column. META-016 should be reframed to specify the admin endpoint on the new association.

No reviewer-vs-reviewer substantive conflict was found — all four axes converge on the same core defects (multitenant pantry scoping, CAS/marker timing, 30-minute window + auto_sync_run_time, target-list ownership, pantry-filter implementation seam, event payload, manual-trigger route). Disagreements are about WHICH layer to fix, not WHETHER to fix.
summary: v1 spec FAILS across all four axes with strong convergence on 7 critical defects. Architecture's 3 critical + 2 high are the correct priority-1 set, but completeness and consistency surface 4 additional critical actions (META-005 pantry-filter seam, META-006 event payload, META-007 manual-trigger route, plus they amplify META-001/META-003 as critical). 18 deduped actions total: 7 critical (P1), 4 high (P2), 6 medium (P3), 1 low (P4). Recommended fix order: META-001 (per-household pantry model — unblocks META-005, META-011, META-016) → META-002 (CAS ordering) → META-003 (run_time + 30-min window) → META-004 (target ownership) → META-005 (pantry filter implementation) → META-006 (event type) → META-007 (manual trigger). No reviewer-vs-reviewer conflicts; all four latent cross-axis tensions resolve cleanly in the merged actions above.
